```python
            agent_y = int(agent_pos_match_class.group(3))
            agent_pos_reliable = True
            output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from 'Yourself a {agent_class}'): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
            output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")
        else:
            # Check for cursor position if 'Yourself a [class]' is not found
            cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
            if cursor_match:
                cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
                cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))

                if is_active_prompt:
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator during active prompt). Using cursor position as best estimate for current focus: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
                    agent_x, agent_y = cursor_x, cursor_y 
                    agent_pos_reliable = True 
                else: 
                    agent_x = cursor_x
                    agent_y = cursor_y
                    agent_pos_reliable = True
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from cursor, no 'Yourself a [class]' indicator): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
                
                agent_full_status_match = re.search(r"Agent the (\w+)\s+St:", observation_text)
                if agent_full_status_match:
                    agent_class = agent_full_status_match.group(1)
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")

            else:
                output_lines.append("Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator and cursor coordinates).")
    else: # Agent is dead
        cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
        if cursor_match:
            cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
            cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
            output_lines.append(f"Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position. Cursor visible at: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position or cursor visible.")


    # 1. Identify items explicitly on the ground (via "You see here:")
    you_see_here_match = re.search(r"You see here:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|\n\nAgent the \w+|$)", observation_text)
    items_on_ground_explicit = []
    if you_see_here_match:
        ground_content = you_see_here_match.group(1).strip()
        if ground_content:
            ground_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", ground_content)
            for char, desc in ground_item_matches:
                items_on_ground_explicit.append(f"{char} - {desc.strip()}")
        
    if items_on_ground_explicit:
        output_lines.append(f"Items explicitly on the ground: {', '.join(items_on_ground_explicit)}")
        output_lines.append("Suggested action for ground items: pickup (to collect these items).")
    else:
        output_lines.append("No specific items explicitly found on the ground.")


    # 2. Extract items mentioned in the message and infer their location.
    items_on_ground_inferred_from_message = []

    if message_content and not is_active_prompt: 
        message_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", message_content)

        for char, desc in message_item_matches:
            candidate_item = f"{char} - {desc.strip()}"
            is_explicitly_on_ground = any(candidate_item == s for s in items_on_ground_explicit)

            if not is_explicitly_on_ground:
                items_on_ground_inferred_from_message.append(candidate_item)
        
    if items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
        output_lines.append(f"Items recognized as on the ground from general message content: {', '.join(items_on_ground_inferred_from_message)}")
        if not items_on_ground_explicit: 
            output_lines.append("Suggested action for these items: pickup (to collect these items, if not already in inventory).")
    elif not items_on_ground_explicit and not is_active_prompt: # Only report this if no explicit ground items either and not an active prompt
        output_lines.append("No specific items recognized as on the ground from general message content.")


    # 4. Check for stairs at current location based on world knowledge and infer actions
    known_stairs_location = (26, 10)
    
    # Check if agent_x and agent_y are not None before comparison
    stairs_present_at_known_location = False
    if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None:
        stairs_present_at_known_location = (agent_x == known_stairs_location[0] and agent_y == known_stairs_location[1])
    
    if stairs_present_at_known_location:
        descent_blocked = False
        description_suffix = ""
        if "You can't go down here" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('You can't go down here' detected)."
        elif "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor' detected)."
        
        if descent_blocked:
            # The more detailed message for stairs blocking is now in the general message classification.
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Blocked {description_suffix} The stairs are currently impassable.")
        else:
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Potentially traversable (known stairs at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}).")

        if not descent_blocked:
            if not items_on_ground_explicit and not items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: down (to descend the stairs).")
            elif items_on_ground_explicit or items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: Pickup items on the ground before attempting to descend stairs.")
    else:
        if agent_pos_reliable: # Only make a statement about not being at stairs if agent position is known
             output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Not at agent's current map location. Known stairs are at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}.")
        else:
             output_lines.append("Stairs descent status: Cannot assess, agent's map position is not reliably determined.")


    # 5. Check for 'apply' and 'quaff' action prompts and directional prompts
    if prompt_type == "apply_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        if choices_match:
            choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
            output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action.")
    elif prompt_type == "directional_input":
        output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for directional input.")
    elif prompt_type == "quaff_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'quaff' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
    elif prompt_type == "yes_no_quit":
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is presenting a Yes/No/Quit style prompt: '{message_content.strip()}'")


    # 6. The "Never mind." message is now handled in the main message classification.
    
    # 8. Extract identified item details from auxiliary_observation if present
    aux_obs_match = re.search(r"auxiliary_observation:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text)
    if aux_obs_match:
        aux_obs_content = aux_obs_match.group(1).strip()
        inventory_match = re.search(r"inventory:\n([\s\S]+)", aux_obs_content)
        if inventory_match:
            inventory_items = inventory_match.group(1).strip().split('\n')
            identified_items = []
            for item in inventory_items:
                if re.match(r"^[a-zA-Z]: .* \([\w\s]+:\d+\)$", item.strip()):
                    identified_items.append(item.strip()) 
            if identified_items:
                output_lines.append(f"Identified inventory items (from auxiliary_observation): {'; '.join(identified_items)}")
            else: # inventory section found, but no identified items match the pattern
                output_lines.append("Auxiliary observation: Inventory section found, but no identified inventory items within it.")
        else: # auxiliary_observation section found, but no explicit 'inventory:' header
            output_lines.append("Auxiliary observation section found, but no inventory details within it.")
    else:
        output_lines.append("No auxiliary_observation section found in the raw observation.")
    
    return "\n".join(output_lines)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    output_lines = []

    # Initialize agent position and status
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    agent_pos_reliable = False
    agent_class = None # Initialize agent_class

    # Extract raw message content first
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    message_content = ""
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
    output_lines.append(f"Raw message content: {message_content}")

    # Check for critical status changes (e.g., death) first, as it affects other perceptions
    is_agent_dead = False
    if "Killed by" in message_content or "You die" in message_content or "You are dead" in message_content:
        output_lines.append("Agent status: dead (death message detected).")
        is_agent_dead = True
    
    # Determine if message content is an active prompt or a general status update (including invalid actions)
    is_active_prompt = False
    prompt_type = None

    if "What do you want to use or apply?" in message_content:
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "apply_item_selection"
    elif re.search(r"In what direction\?", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "directional_input"
    elif re.search(r"What do you want to quaff\? \[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "quaff_item_selection"
    elif re.search(r"\[[ynqYNQ]+\]", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "yes_no_quit"
    
    if is_active_prompt:
        output_lines.append(f"Message content is an active prompt (type: {prompt_type}).")
    else:
        # Refined classification for non-active-prompt messages
        if "You can't go down here" in message_content or "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates stairs are blocked (potentially a persistent message from a previous turn if not a response to 'down').")
        elif "Never mind." in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates previous action/prompt cancelled ('Never mind.' detected).")
        elif "What?" in message_content or "Unknown command" in message_content or "I don't understand that." in message_content or "You can't do that." in message_content or "Your previous output did not contain a valid action. Retry" in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates an invalid or unrecognized action was attempted (message persists, no turn consumed).")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Message content is a general status update (not an active prompt).")


    # 3. Extract agent's current position and class: prioritize "Yourself a [class]", then "cursor"
    if not is_agent_dead:
        agent_pos_match_class = re.search(r"Yourself a (\w+)\n\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
        if agent_pos_match_class:
            agent_class = agent_pos_match_class.group(1)
            agent_x = int(agent_pos_match_class.group(2))
            agent_y = int(agent_pos_match_class.group(3))
            agent_pos_reliable = True
            output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from 'Yourself a {agent_class}'): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
            output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")
        else:
            # Check for cursor position if 'Yourself a [class]' is not found
            cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
            if cursor_match:
                cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
                cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))

                if is_active_prompt:
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator during active prompt). Using cursor position as best estimate for current focus: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
                    agent_x, agent_y = cursor_x, cursor_y 
                    agent_pos_reliable = True 
                else: 
                    agent_x = cursor_x
                    agent_y = cursor_y
                    agent_pos_reliable = True
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from cursor, no 'Yourself a [class]' indicator): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
                
                agent_full_status_match = re.search(r"Agent the (\w+)\s+St:", observation_text)
                if agent_full_status_match:
                    agent_class = agent_full_status_match.group(1)
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")

            else:
                output_lines.append("Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator and cursor coordinates).")
    else: # Agent is dead
        cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
        if cursor_match:
            cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
            cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
            output_lines.append(f"Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position. Cursor visible at: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position or cursor visible.")


    # 1. Identify items explicitly on the ground (via "You see here:")
    you_see_here_match = re.search(r"You see here:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|\n\nAgent the \w+|$)", observation_text)
    items_on_ground_explicit = []
    if you_see_here_match:
        ground_content = you_see_here_match.group(1).strip()
        if ground_content:
            ground_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", ground_content)
            for char, desc in ground_item_matches:
                items_on_ground_explicit.append(f"{char} - {desc.strip()}")
        
    if items_on_ground_explicit:
        output_lines.append(f"Items explicitly on the ground: {', '.join(items_on_ground_explicit)}")
        output_lines.append("Suggested action for ground items: pickup (to collect these items).")
    else:
        output_lines.append("No specific items explicitly found on the ground.")


    # 2. Extract items mentioned in the message and infer their location.
    items_on_ground_inferred_from_message = []

    # Only parse items from message content if it's not an active prompt, to avoid misinterpreting prompt choices
    # as items on the ground. Also, general messages that *are* also "retry" messages should not be parsed for items.
    is_invalid_action_message = "Your previous output did not contain a valid action. Retry" in message_content or \
                                "What?" in message_content or "Unknown command" in message_content or \
                                "I don't understand that." in message_content or "You can't do that." in message_content

    if message_content and not is_active_prompt and not is_invalid_action_message: 
        message_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", message_content)

        for char, desc in message_item_matches:
            candidate_item = f"{char} - {desc.strip()}"
            is_explicitly_on_ground = any(candidate_item == s for s in items_on_ground_explicit)

            if not is_explicitly_on_ground: # Only infer if not already explicitly on ground.
                items_on_ground_inferred_from_message.append(candidate_item)
        
    if items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
        output_lines.append(f"Items recognized as on the ground from general message content: {', '.join(items_on_ground_inferred_from_message)}")
        if not items_on_ground_explicit: 
            output_lines.append("Suggested action for these items: pickup (to collect these items, if not already in inventory).")
    elif not items_on_ground_explicit: # Only report 'no specific items' if no explicit items were found either.
        output_lines.append("No specific items recognized as on the ground from general message content.")


    # 4. Check for stairs at current location based on world knowledge and infer actions
    known_stairs_location = (26, 10)
    stairs_present_at_known_location = (agent_x == known_stairs_location[0] and agent_y == known_stairs_location[1]) if agent_pos_reliable else False

    if stairs_present_at_known_location:
        descent_blocked = False
        description_suffix = ""
        if "You can't go down here" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('You can't go down here' detected)."
        elif "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor' detected)."
        
        if descent_blocked:
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Blocked {description_suffix} The stairs are currently impassable.")
        else:
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Potentially traversable (known stairs at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}).")

        if not descent_blocked:
            if not items_on_ground_explicit and not items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: down (to descend the stairs).")
            elif items_on_ground_explicit or items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: Pickup items on the ground before attempting to descend stairs.")
    else:
        if agent_pos_reliable:
             output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Not at agent's current map location. Known stairs are at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}.")
        else:
             output_lines.append("Stairs descent status: Not reliably determined at agent's map location or unknown.")


    # 5. Check for 'apply' and 'quaff' action prompts and directional prompts - already handled in the main prompt detection.
    # The dedicated lines for prompting for item selection or direction are already under the `if prompt_type == ...` blocks.
    if prompt_type == "apply_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        if choices_match:
            choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
            output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action.")
    elif prompt_type == "directional_input":
        # The message content for directional input is often just "In what direction?".
        output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for directional input.")
    elif prompt_type == "quaff_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        if choices_match: # Added check for choices_match for consistency.
            choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
            output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'quaff' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'quaff' action.")
    elif prompt_type == "yes_no_quit":
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is presenting a Yes/No/Quit style prompt: '{message_content.strip()}'")


    # 8. Extract identified item details from auxiliary_observation if present
    aux_obs_match = re.search(r"auxiliary_observation:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text)
    if aux_obs_match:
        aux_obs_content = aux_obs_match.group(1).strip()
        inventory_match = re.search(r"inventory:\n([\s\S]+)", aux_obs_content)
        if inventory_match:
            inventory_items = inventory_match.group(1).strip().split('\n')
            identified_items = []
            for item in inventory_items:
                # Regex for identified items: starts with letter: followed by any chars, then (BUC:charge)
                if re.match(r"^[a-zA-Z]: .* (\(.\s*:\d+\))$", item.strip()): # e.g., 'f: a blessed frost horn (0:3)'
                    identified_items.append(item.strip()) 
                # Also include items identified only via their name (without BUC:charge yet).
                # This ensures inventory items are caught even if not fully identified with (buc:charge)
                elif re.match(r"^[a-zA-Z]: .*$", item.strip()):
                     identified_items.append(item.strip())

            if identified_items:
                output_lines.append(f"Identified inventory items (from auxiliary_observation): {'; '.join(identified_items)}")
            else:
                output_lines.append("Auxiliary observation contained an inventory section, but no identified items were found.")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Auxiliary observation section present, but no inventory subsection found.")
    else:
        output_lines.append("No auxiliary_observation section found in the raw observation.")


    return "\n".join(output_lines)
```

feedback_history.qa_feedback_prompt:
You are evaluating whether an agent's predicted answers to questions about an environment are correct.

For each question below, you are given:
- The question about the environment
- The agent's reasoning and predicted answer
- The correct answer with supporting evidence

Evaluate whether the prediction is:
- CORRECT: the agent's answer matches the correct answer
- INCORRECT: the agent's answer contradicts the correct answer
- INCONCLUSIVE: the agent's prediction was MISSING or the question is ambiguous

For INCORRECT predictions, explain what knowledge the agent was missing or had wrong.

--- Question 1 ---
Question: If the agent uses the 'pickup' action when items 'n' and 'o' are explicitly listed on the ground, will those items be added to the agent's inventory?
Agent's reasoning: 
Agent's predicted answer: MISSING
Correct answer: YES
Evidence for correct answer: In step 9, the perception output states: "Items recognized as on the ground from message content: n - a horn, o - a curved wand". After the `pickup` action, the `auxiliary_observation` in the resulting state shows "n: a horn" and "o: a curved wand" in the inventory.
---

--- Question 2 ---
Question: If the agent uses the 'pickup' action when items 'n' and 'o' are explicitly listed on the ground, will a turn be consumed?
Agent's reasoning: 
Agent's predicted answer: MISSING
Correct answer: YES
Evidence for correct answer: In step 9, the agent performs `pickup`. The `message` in the resulting state changed from describing the items on the ground to "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor.", indicating that a turn was consumed for the action to complete.
---

--- Question 3 ---
Question: If the agent uses the 'pickup' action to pick up 'n - a horn', will the auxiliary observation immediately identify it as a "blessed frost horn" if it is indeed the same type as the previously identified horn 'f'?
Agent's reasoning: 
Agent's predicted answer: MISSING
Correct answer: NO
Evidence for correct answer: In step 8 (previous episode), item 'f' was identified as "f: a blessed frost horn (0:3)". In step 9, after picking up 'n - a horn', the item in the inventory is listed as "n: a horn", not "blessed frost horn", indicating its identification status reset or is unknown for the new character.
---

--- Question 4 ---
Question: Does using the 'pickup' action on items 'n' and 'o' cause any other message to appear beyond the inventory update?
Agent's reasoning: 
Agent's predicted answer: MISSING
Correct answer: NO
Evidence for correct answer: In step 9, after the `pickup` action, the message presented in the state is "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor.", and there is no direct message confirming the pickup of items 'n' and 'o'.
---

--- Question 5 ---
Question: Does the general message "No specific items explicitly found on the ground. Items recognized as on the ground from message content: n - a horn, o - a curved wand" indicate that the items 'n' and 'o' are available to be picked up by the `pickup` action?
Agent's reasoning: 
Agent's predicted answer: MISSING
Correct answer: YES
Evidence for correct answer: In step 9, the perception output includes this message, and the subsequent `pickup` action successfully moved items 'n' and 'o' into the agent's inventory, as seen in the `auxiliary_observation` of the resulting state (step 10).
---

--- Question 6 ---
Question: Does the identification status of items (like horns or wands) reset upon a new character respawn after a previous character's death?
Agent's reasoning: 
Agent's predicted answer: MISSING
Correct answer: YES
Evidence for correct answer: After the "caveman" died in step 8, a new character ("wizard") appeared in step 9. Item 'f' (horn) was identified as "blessed frost horn" for the caveman, but the new horn 'n' for the wizard is simply "a horn", confirming a reset in identification knowledge between characters.
---

--- Question 7 ---
Question: Does the cursor's location or object ("Yourself a caveman", "Yourself a wizard") consistently disappear from the state observation when the environment is prompting for user input (e.g., item selection, improvisation choice, direction)?
Agent's reasoning: 
Agent's predicted answer: MISSING
Correct answer: YES
Evidence for correct answer: In steps 3, 4, 6, 7, and 8, when prompts requiring user input are active, the text-based description of the agent's class and position (e.g., "Yourself a caveman (x=26, y=10)") that is typically accompanied by the `cursor` observation, is replaced with a generic cursor observation at `(x=[some_value], y=0)` and no "Yourself a [class]" text.
---

--- Question 8 ---
Question: Does the message "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" always prevent the use of the `down` action on the current dungeon level?
Agent's reasoning: 
Agent's predicted answer: MISSING
Correct answer: YES
Evidence for correct answer: In step 1, the message "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor." appeared. Subsequently, in step 2, attempting the `down` action resulted in "You can't go down here." A similar sequence occurred in steps 10 and 11. This consistently demonstrates that this message accompanies or directly causes the inability to descend stairs.

--- Question 9 ---
Question: After a successful `pickup` action, will the environment always provide a direct message confirming the pickup of specific items, or is the inventory update the sole confirmation?
Agent's reasoning: 
Agent's predicted answer: MISSING
Correct answer: NO

In the observation for step 10, the message is "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor.", and there is no direct message confirming the pickup of items 'n' and 'o'. The only confirmation is their presence in the `auxiliary_observation` inventory.

In step 2 (also step 11), the message changed to "You can't go down here." This establishes a two-stage message sequence for blocked stairs when attempting descent.

feedback_history.qa_forward_prompt:
You are an agent that has been interacting with an environment. Based on your current knowledge, answer each question below with YES or NO.

=== YOUR KNOWLEDGE ===
<world_knowledge>
- Messages in the format "letter - item_description" without an explicit "You see here:" indicate items on the floor that can be picked up and added to inventory if not already present.
- The `pickup` action successfully moves items from the ground into the agent's inventory and consumes a turn. After a `pickup` action, the environment does not provide a direct message confirming the pickup of specific items; confirmation is via their presence in the `auxiliary_observation` inventory.
- The message "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" appears if stairs are present but non-functional for descent. Attempting `down` after this message will then result in "You can't go down here." This is a two-stage message sequence confirming blocked stairs and consistently prevents the `down` action.
- The `apply` action initiates a prompt to select an item from inventory (e.g., "What do you want to use or apply? [fg or ?*]").
- Responding 'n' or 'q' to "Improvise? [ynq]" immediately results in the "Never mind." message and cancels the `apply` action. Responding 'y' allows the application to proceed.
- When selecting an item with a letter at an item selection prompt (e.g., for `apply` or `quaff`), the environment implicitly confirms the selection by immediately proceeding to a follow-up message (e.g., "Improvise? [ynq]" or "In what direction?").
- The `apply` action, if it proceeds after "Improvise? [ynq]" (by selecting 'y'), may require directional input (e.g., "In what direction?"). Providing this directional input, including cardinal directions like 'east' or 'west', consumes a turn. If the applied item has an immediate and significant effect, such as causing damage or death, this effect results in an explicit message detailing its nature and impact.
- The `quaff` action, similar to `apply`, first requires the action command (`quaff`) and then prompts for an item selection from inventory (e.g., "What do you want to drink? [fgh or ?*]"). Specifying the item letter directly with the `quaff` command (e.g., `quaff f`) is not a valid action.
- Upon character death, the game immediately resets to a new character generation, and item identification status and inventory reset. A new character (with different class, stats, and starting inventory) replaces the previous character.
- Identified items (e.g., in the inventory section of `auxiliary_observation`) list their specific type, BUC status, and charge count (e.g., 'f: a blessed frost horn (0:3)'). The presence of an item in the `auxiliary_observation` in this format indicates it is identified.
- If an invalid action command is provided, an error message such as "Your previous output did not contain a valid action. Retry" appears, but the previous environment message persists, and a turn is not consumed.
</world_knowledge>
<policy>
- When interpreting messages, items in 'letter - item_description' format without a "You see here:" prefix are generally on the ground and can be picked up if not in inventory.
- Do not attempt to `pickup` items already in the inventory.
- Treat "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" and "You can't go down here" as blocking messages for the `down` action; do not attempt to go down if these messages are present.
- Use the '@' symbol and the "Yourself a [class] (x,y)" line for determining the agent's current true location. When "Yourself a [class]" is absent, the cursor position (x,y) serves as the best estimate, specifically during active prompts where the main map position may not be representative. During active prompts (e.g., for `apply` or `quaff` item selection, or "Improvise? [ynq]", or directional input), the "Yourself a [class]" text-based description of the agent and its map coordinates consistently disappears from the observation.
- When prompted with "Improvise? [ynq]" after initiating `apply` and selecting an item, always choose 'y' to proceed with the action and observe its effects.
- Be extremely cautious when `apply`ing unidentified items, particularly those requiring a direction, as they can have immediate and potentially lethal consequences, including instant death. Consider moving to a safe, empty space and choosing a direction away from the agent before applying such items.
- When performing actions that might require further input (e.g., `quaff` or `apply`), ensure the initial action is correctly formatted (e.g., just `quaff` or `apply` instead of `quaff f` or `apply f`) to receive the subsequent prompt.
- If the perception output includes "Suggested action: Pickup items on the ground before attempting to descend stairs", prioritize picking up items over attempting to descend.
</policy>
=== END YOUR KNOWLEDGE ===

For each question, reason step-by-step about what your knowledge says (or doesn't say), then give your answer.

Providing this directional input, including cardinal directions like 'east' or 'west', consumes a turn. If the applied item has an immediate and significant effect, such as causing damage or death, this effect results in an explicit message detailing its nature and impact.
- The `quaff` action, similar to `apply`, first requires the action command (`quaff`) and then prompts for an item selection from inventory. Specifying the item letter directly with the `quaff` command (e.g., `quaff f`) is not a valid action.
- Upon character death, the game immediately resets to a new character generation, and item identification status and inventory reset. A new character (with different class, stats, and starting inventory) replaces the previous character.

=== CURRENT PERCEPTION MODULE ===
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    output_lines = []

    # Initialize agent position and status
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    agent_pos_reliable = False
    agent_class = None # Initialize agent_class

    # Extract raw message content first
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    message_content = ""
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
    output_lines.append(f"Raw message content: {message_content}")

    # Check for critical status changes (e.g., death) first, as it affects other perceptions
    is_agent_dead = False
    if "Killed by" in message_content or "You die" in message_content or "You are dead" in message_content:
        output_lines.append("Agent status: dead (death message detected).")
        is_agent_dead = True
    
    # Determine if message content is an active prompt or a general status update (including invalid actions)
    is_active_prompt = False
    prompt_type = None

    if "What do you want to use or apply?" in message_content:
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "apply_item_selection"
    elif re.search(r"In what direction\?", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "directional_input"
    elif re.search(r"What do you want to quaff\? \[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "quaff_item_selection"
    elif re.search(r"\[[ynqYNQ]+\]", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "yes_no_quit"
    
    if is_active_prompt:
        output_lines.append(f"Message content is an active prompt.")
    else:
        # Refined classification for non-active-prompt messages
        if "You can't go down here" in message_content or "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates stairs are blocked (potentially a persistent message from a previous turn if not a response to 'down').")
        elif "Never mind." in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates previous action/prompt cancelled ('Never mind.' detected).")
        elif "What?" in message_content or "Unknown command" in message_content or "I don't understand that." in message_content or "You can't do that." in message_content or "Your previous output did not contain a valid action. Retry" in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates an invalid or unrecognized action was attempted (message persists, no turn consumed).")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Message content is a general status update (not an active prompt).")


    # 3. Extract agent's current position and class: prioritize "Yourself a [class]", then "cursor"
    if not is_agent_dead:
        agent_pos_match_class = re.search(r"Yourself a (\w+)\n\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
        if agent_pos_match_class:
            agent_class = agent_pos_match_class.group(1)
            agent_x = int(agent_pos_match_class.group(2))
            agent_y = int(agent_pos_match_class.group(3))
            agent_pos_reliable = True
            output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from 'Yourself a {agent_class}'): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
            output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")
        else:
            # Check for cursor position if 'Yourself a [class]' is not found
            cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
            if cursor_match:
                cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
                cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))

                if is_active_prompt:
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator during active prompt). Cursor position (likely for active prompt): (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
                    agent_x, agent_y = cursor_x, cursor_y 
                    agent_pos_reliable = True 
                else: 
                    agent_x = cursor_x
                    agent_y = cursor_y
                    agent_pos_reliable = True
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from cursor, no 'Yourself a [class]' indicator): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
                
                agent_full_status_match = re.search(r"Agent the (\w+)\s+St:", observation_text)
                if agent_full_status_match:
                    agent_class = agent_full_status_match.group(1)
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")

            else:
                output_lines.append("Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator and cursor coordinates).")
    else: # Agent is dead
        cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
        if cursor_match:
            cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
            cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
            output_lines.append(f"Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position. Cursor visible at: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position or cursor visible.")


    # 1. Identify items explicitly on the ground (via "You see here:")
    you_see_here_match = re.search(r"You see here:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|\n\nAgent the \w+|$)", observation_text)
    items_on_ground_explicit = []
    if you_see_here_match:
        ground_content = you_see_here_match.group(1).strip()
        if ground_content:
            ground_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", ground_content)
            for char, desc in ground_item_matches:
                items_on_ground_explicit.append(f"{char} - {desc.strip()}")
        
    if items_on_ground_explicit:
        output_lines.append(f"Items explicitly on the ground: {', '.join(items_on_ground_explicit)}")
        output_lines.append("Suggested action for ground items: pickup (to collect these items).")
    else:
        output_lines.append("No specific items explicitly found on the ground.")


    # 2. Extract items mentioned in the message and infer their location.
    items_on_ground_inferred_from_message = []

    if message_content and not is_active_prompt: 
        message_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", message_content)

        for char, desc in message_item_matches:
            candidate_item = f"{char} - {desc.strip()}"
            is_explicitly_on_ground = any(candidate_item == s for s in items_on_ground_explicit)

            if not is_explicitly_on_ground:
                items_on_ground_inferred_from_message.append(candidate_item)
        
    if items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
        output_lines.append(f"Items recognized as on the ground from message content (no active prompt): {', '.join(items_on_ground_inferred_from_message)}")
        if not items_on_ground_explicit: 
            output_lines.append("Suggested action for these items: pickup (to collect these items, if not already in inventory).")
    elif not items_on_ground_explicit: 
        output_lines.append("No specific items recognized as on the ground from message content (no active prompt).")


    # 4. Check for stairs at current location based on world knowledge and infer actions
    known_stairs_location = (26, 10)
    stairs_present_at_known_location = (agent_x == known_stairs_location[0] and agent_y == known_stairs_location[1]) if agent_pos_reliable else False

    if stairs_present_at_known_location:
        descent_blocked = False
        description_suffix = ""
        if "You can't go down here" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('You can't go down here' detected)."
        elif "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor' detected)."
        
        if descent_blocked:
            # The more detailed message for stairs blocking is now in the general message classification.
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Blocked {description_suffix} The stairs are currently impassable.")
        else:
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Potentially traversable (known stairs at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}).")

        if not descent_blocked:
            if not items_on_ground_explicit and not items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: down (to descend the stairs).")
            elif items_on_ground_explicit or items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: Pickup items on the ground before attempting to descend stairs.")
    else:
        if agent_pos_reliable:
             output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Not at agent's current location (known stairs at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}).")
        else:
             output_lines.append("Stairs descent status: Not at agent's reliably determined location or unknown.")


    # 5. Check for 'apply' and 'quaff' action prompts and directional prompts
    if prompt_type == "apply_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        if choices_match:
            choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
            output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action.")
    elif prompt_type == "directional_input":
        output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for directional input.")
    elif prompt_type == "quaff_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'quaff' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
    elif prompt_type == "yes_no_quit":
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is presenting a Yes/No/Quit style prompt: '{message_content.strip()}'")


    # 6. The "Never mind." message is now handled in the main message classification.
    
    # 8. Extract identified item details from auxiliary_observation if present
    aux_obs_match = re.search(r"auxiliary_observation:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text)
    if aux_obs_match:
        aux_obs_content = aux_obs_match.group(1).strip()
        inventory_match = re.search(r"inventory:\n([\s\S]+)", aux_obs_content)
        if inventory_match:
            inventory_items = inventory_match.group(1).strip().split('\n')
            identified_items = []
            for item in inventory_items:
                if re.match(r"^[a-zA-Z]: .* \([\w\s]+:\d+\)$", item.strip()):
                    identified_items.append(item.strip()) 
            if identified_items:
                output_lines.append(f"Identified inventory items (from auxiliary_observation): {'; '.join(identified_items)}")


    return "\n".join(output_lines)
=== END CURRENT PERCEPTION MODULE ===

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    output_lines = []

    # Initialize agent position and status
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    agent_pos_reliable = False
    agent_class = None # Initialize agent_class

    # Extract raw message content first
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    message_content = ""
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
    output_lines.append(f"Raw message content: {message_content}")

    # Check for critical status changes (e.g., death) first, as it affects other perceptions
    is_agent_dead = False
    if "Killed by" in message_content or "You die" in message_content or "You are dead" in message_content:
        output_lines.append("Agent status: dead (death message detected).")
        is_agent_dead = True
    
    # Determine if message content is an active prompt or a general status update (including invalid actions)
    is_active_prompt = False
    prompt_type = None
    is_invalid_action_message = False

    if "What do you want to use or apply?" in message_content:
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "apply_item_selection"
    elif re.search(r"In what direction\?", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "directional_input"
    elif re.search(r"What do you want to (quaff|drink)\? \[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content): # Updated regex for quaff/drink
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "quaff_item_selection"
    elif re.search(r"\[[ynqYNQ]+\]", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "yes_no_quit"
    
    # Check for invalid action messages more robustly
    invalid_action_patterns = [
        "What?", "Unknown command", "I don't understand that.", "You can't do that.",
        "Your previous output did not contain a valid action. Retry"
    ]
    if any(pattern in message_content for pattern in invalid_action_patterns):
        is_invalid_action_message = True

    if is_active_prompt:
        output_lines.append(f"Message content is an active prompt (type: {prompt_type}).")
    elif is_invalid_action_message: # Prioritize invalid action message over general status if both could apply
        output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates an invalid or unrecognized action was attempted (message persists, no turn consumed).")
    else:
        # Refined classification for non-active-prompt messages
        if "You can't go down here" in message_content or "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates stairs are blocked (potentially a persistent message from a previous turn if not a response to 'down').")
        elif "Never mind." in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates previous action/prompt cancelled ('Never mind.' detected).")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Message content is a general status update (not an active prompt).")


    # 3. Extract agent's current position and class: prioritize "Yourself a [class]", then "cursor"
    if not is_agent_dead:
        agent_pos_match_class = re.search(r"Yourself a (\w+)\n\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
        if agent_pos_match_class:
            agent_class = agent_pos_match_class.group(1)
            agent_x = int(agent_pos_match_class.group(2))
            agent_y = int(agent_pos_match_class.group(3))
            agent_pos_reliable = True
            output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from 'Yourself a {agent_class}'): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
            output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")
        else:
            # Check for cursor position if 'Yourself a [class]' is not found
            cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
            if cursor_match:
                cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
                cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))

                if is_active_prompt:
                    # Per policy: "When "Yourself a [class]" is absent, the cursor position (x,y) serves as the best estimate, specifically during active prompts where the main map position may not be representative."
                    # This means we should use cursor as agent_x,y, but note it might be a prompt's cursor.
                    agent_x = cursor_x
                    agent_y = cursor_y
                    agent_pos_reliable = True
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator during active prompt). Using cursor position as best estimate: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
                else: 
                    agent_x = cursor_x
                    agent_y = cursor_y
                    agent_pos_reliable = True
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from cursor, no 'Yourself a [class]' indicator): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
                
                agent_full_status_match = re.search(r"Agent the (\w+)\s+St:", observation_text)
                if agent_full_status_match:
                    agent_class = agent_full_status_match.group(1)
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")

            else:
                output_lines.append("Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator and cursor coordinates).")
    else: # Agent is dead
        cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
        if cursor_match:
            cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
            cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
            output_lines.append(f"Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position. Cursor visible at: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position or cursor visible.")


    # 1. Identify items explicitly on the ground (via "You see here:")
    you_see_here_match = re.search(r"You see here:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|\n\nAgent the \w+|$)", observation_text)
    items_on_ground_explicit = []
    if you_see_here_match:
        ground_content = you_see_here_match.group(1).strip()
        if ground_content:
            ground_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", ground_content)
            for char, desc in ground_item_matches:
                items_on_ground_explicit.append(f"{char} - {desc.strip()}")
        
    if items_on_ground_explicit:
        output_lines.append(f"Items explicitly on the ground: {', '.join(items_on_ground_explicit)}")
        output_lines.append("Suggested action for ground items: pickup (to collect these items).")
    else:
        output_lines.append("No specific items explicitly found on the ground.")


    # 2. Extract items mentioned in the message and infer their location.
    items_on_ground_inferred_from_message = []

    if message_content and not is_active_prompt: 
        message_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", message_content)

        for char, desc in message_item_matches:
            candidate_item = f"{char} - {desc.strip( )}"
            is_explicitly_on_ground = any(candidate_item == s for s in items_on_ground_explicit)

            if not is_explicitly_on_ground:
                items_on_ground_inferred_from_message.append(candidate_item)
        
    if items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
        output_lines.append(f"Items recognized as on the ground from message content (no active prompt): {', '.join(items_on_ground_inferred_from_message)}")
        if not items_on_ground_explicit: 
            output_lines.append("Suggested action for these items: pickup (to collect these items, if not already in inventory).")
    elif not items_on_ground_explicit and not is_active_prompt and not is_invalid_action_message: # Only state this if not an active prompt or invalid action message
        output_lines.append("No specific items recognized as on the ground from general message content.")


    # 4. Check for stairs at current location based on world knowledge and infer actions
    known_stairs_location = (26, 10)
    stairs_present_at_known_location = (agent_x == known_stairs_location[0] and agent_y == known_stairs_location[1]) if agent_pos_reliable else False

    if stairs_present_at_known_location:
        descent_blocked = False
        description_suffix = ""
        if "You can't go down here" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('You can't go down here' detected)."
        elif "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor' detected)."
        
        if descent_blocked:
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Blocked {description_suffix} The stairs are currently impassable.")
        else:
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Potentially traversable (known stairs at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}).")

        if not descent_blocked:
            if not items_on_ground_explicit and not items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: down (to descend the stairs).")
            elif items_on_ground_explicit or items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: Pickup items on the ground before attempting to descend stairs.")
    else:
        if agent_pos_reliable:
             output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Not at agent's current map location. Known stairs are at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}.")
        else:
             output_lines.append("Stairs descent status: Not at agent's reliably determined map location or unknown.")


    # 5. Check for 'apply' and 'quaff' action prompts and directional prompts
    if prompt_type == "apply_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        if choices_match:
            choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
            output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action.")
    elif prompt_type == "directional_input":
        output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for directional input.")
    elif prompt_type == "quaff_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'quaff' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
    elif prompt_type == "yes_no_quit":
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is presenting a Yes/No/Quit style prompt: '{message_content.strip()}'")


    # 6. The "Never mind." message is now handled in the main message classification.
    
    # 8. Extract identified item details from auxiliary_observation if present
    aux_obs_match = re.search(r"auxiliary_observation:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text)
    if aux_obs_match:
        aux_obs_content = aux_obs_match.group(1).strip()
        
        # Add a specific note if the action was invalid
        if is_invalid_action_message:
            output_lines.append("Auxiliary observation (including inventory) is explicitly provided and represents the state *before* the invalid action, as no turn was consumed.")
        
        inventory_match = re.search(r"inventory:\n([\s\S]+)", aux_obs_content)
        if inventory_match:
            inventory_items = inventory_match.group(1).strip().split('\n')
            identified_items = []
            for item in inventory_items:
                if re.match(r"^[a-zA-Z]: .* \([\w\s]+:\d+\)$", item.strip()):
                    identified_items.append(item.strip()) 
            if identified_items:
                output_lines.append(f"Identified inventory items (from auxiliary_observation): {'; '.join(identified_items)}")
            else:
                output_lines.append("No identified inventory items in auxiliary_observation.")

        else: # aux_obs_match present but no inventory section
            output_lines.append("Auxiliary observation present, but no inventory section found.")
    else:
        # If no aux_obs_match, and an invalid action occurred, state that as well
        if is_invalid_action_message:
            output_lines.append("No auxiliary observation (including inventory) provided. This is unexpected for an invalid action, but implies state is unchanged.")
        else:
            output_lines.append("No auxiliary_observation section found.")


    return "\n".join(output_lines)
```

```python
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator during active prompt). Using cursor position as best estimate for current focus: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
                else: 
                    agent_x = cursor_x
                    agent_y = cursor_y
                    agent_pos_reliable = True
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from cursor, no 'Yourself a [class]' indicator): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
                
                agent_full_status_match = re.search(r"Agent the (\w+)\s+St:", observation_text)
                if agent_full_status_match:
                    agent_class = agent_full_status_match.group(1)
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")

            else:
                output_lines.append("Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator and cursor coordinates).")
    else: # Agent is dead
        cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
        if cursor_match:
            cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
            cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
            output_lines.append(f"Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position. Cursor visible at: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position or cursor visible.")


    # 1. Identify items explicitly on the ground (via "You see here:")
    you_see_here_match = re.search(r"You see here:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|\n\nAgent the \w+|$)", observation_text)
    items_on_ground_explicit = []
    if you_see_here_match:
        ground_content = you_see_here_match.group(1).strip()
        if ground_content:
            ground_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", ground_content)
            for char, desc in ground_item_matches:
                items_on_ground_explicit.append(f"{char} - {desc.strip()}")
        
    if items_on_ground_explicit:
        output_lines.append(f"Items explicitly on the ground: {', '.join(items_on_ground_explicit)}")
        output_lines.append("Suggested action for ground items: pickup (to collect these items).")
    else:
        output_lines.append("No specific items explicitly found on the ground.")


    # 2. Extract items mentioned in the message and infer their location.
    items_on_ground_inferred_from_message = []

    if message_content and not is_active_prompt: 
        message_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", message_content)

        for char, desc in message_item_matches:
            candidate_item = f"{char} - {desc.strip( )}"
            is_explicitly_on_ground = any(candidate_item == s for s in items_on_ground_explicit)

            if not is_explicitly_on_ground:
                items_on_ground_inferred_from_message.append(candidate_item)
        
    if items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
        output_lines.append(f"Items recognized as on the ground from message content (no active prompt): {', '.join(items_on_ground_inferred_from_message)}")
        if not items_on_ground_explicit: 
            output_lines.append("Suggested action for these items: pickup (to collect these items, if not already in inventory).")
    elif not items_on_ground_explicit and not is_active_prompt and not is_invalid_action_message: # Only state this if not an active prompt or invalid action message
        output_lines.append("No specific items recognized as on the ground from general message content.")


    # 4. Check for stairs at current location based on world knowledge and infer actions
    known_stairs_location = (26, 10)
    stairs_present_at_known_location = (agent_x == known_stairs_location[0] and agent_y == known_stairs_location[1]) if agent_pos_reliable else False

    if stairs_present_at_known_location:
        descent_blocked = False
        description_suffix = ""
        if "You can't go down here" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('You can't go down here' detected)."
        elif "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor' detected)."
        
        if descent_blocked:
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Blocked {description_suffix} The stairs are currently impassable.")
        else:
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Potentially traversable (known stairs at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}).")

        if not descent_blocked:
            if not items_on_ground_explicit and not items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: down (to descend the stairs).")
            elif items_on_ground_explicit or items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: Pickup items on the ground before attempting to descend stairs.")
    else:
        if agent_pos_reliable:
             output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Not at agent's current map location. Known stairs are at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}.")
        else:
             output_lines.append("Stairs descent status: Not at agent's reliably determined map location or unknown.")


    # 5. Check for 'apply' and 'quaff' action prompts and directional prompts
    if prompt_type == "apply_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        if choices_match:
            choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
            output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action.")
    elif prompt_type == "directional_input":
        output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for directional input.")
    elif prompt_type == "quaff_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'quaff' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
    elif prompt_type == "yes_no_quit":
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is presenting a Yes/No/Quit style prompt: '{message_content.strip()}'")


    # 6. The "Never mind." message is now handled in the main message classification.
    
    # 8. Extract identified item details from auxiliary_observation if present
    aux_obs_match = re.search(r"auxiliary_observation:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text)
    if aux_obs_match:
        aux_obs_content = aux_obs_match.group(1).strip()
        
        # Add a specific note if the action was invalid
        if is_invalid_action_message:
            output_lines.append("Auxiliary observation (including inventory) is explicitly provided and represents the state *before* the invalid action, as no turn was consumed.")
        
        inventory_match = re.search(r"inventory:\n([\s\S]+)", aux_obs_content)
        if inventory_match:
            inventory_items = inventory_match.group(1).strip().split('\n')
            identified_items = []
            for item in inventory_items:
                if re.match(r"^[a-zA-Z]: .* \([\w\s]+:\d+\)$", item.strip()):
                    identified_items.append(item.strip()) 
            if identified_items:
                output_lines.append(f"Identified inventory items (from auxiliary_observation): {'; '.join(identified_items)}")
            else:
                output_lines.append("No identified inventory items in auxiliary_observation.")

        else: # aux_obs_match present but no inventory section
            output_lines.append("Auxiliary observation present, but no inventory section found.")
    else:
        # If no aux_obs_match, and an invalid action occurred, state that as well
        if is_invalid_action_message:
            output_lines.append("No auxiliary observation (including inventory) provided. This indicates state is unchanged from prior, but is an unusual omission.")
        else:
            output_lines.append("No auxiliary_observation section found.")


    return "\n".join(output_lines)
```

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    output_lines = []

    # Initialize agent position and status
    agent_x, agent_y = None, None
    agent_pos_reliable = False
    agent_class = None # Initialize agent_class

    # Extract raw message content first
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    message_content = ""
    if message_match:
        message_content = message_match.group(1).strip()
    output_lines.append(f"Raw message content: {message_content}")

    # Check for critical status changes (e.g., death) first, as it affects other perceptions
    is_agent_dead = False
    if "Killed by" in message_content or "You die" in message_content or "You are dead" in message_content:
        output_lines.append("Agent status: dead (death message detected).")
        is_agent_dead = True
    
    # Determine if message content is an active prompt or a general status update (including invalid actions)
    is_active_prompt = False
    prompt_type = None

    if "What do you want to use or apply?" in message_content:
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "apply_item_selection"
    elif re.search(r"In what direction\?", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "directional_input"
    elif re.search(r"What do you want to quaff\? \[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "quaff_item_selection"
    elif re.search(r"\[[ynqYNQ]+\]", message_content):
        is_active_prompt = True
        prompt_type = "yes_no_quit"
    
    if is_active_prompt:
        output_lines.append(f"Message content is an active prompt (type: {prompt_type}).")
    else:
        # Refined classification for non-active-prompt messages
        if "You can't go down here" in message_content or "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates stairs are blocked (potentially a persistent message from a previous turn if not a response to 'down').")
        elif "Never mind." in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates previous action/prompt cancelled ('Never mind.' detected).")
        elif "What?" in message_content or "Unknown command" in message_content or "I don't understand that." in message_content or "You can't do that." in message_content or "Your previous output did not contain a valid action. Retry" in message_content:
             output_lines.append(f"Message content indicates an invalid or unrecognized action was attempted (message persists, no turn consumed).")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Message content is a general status update (not an active prompt).")


    # 3. Extract agent's current position and class: prioritize "Yourself a [class]", then "cursor"
    if not is_agent_dead:
        agent_pos_match_class = re.search(r"Yourself a (\w+)\n\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
        if agent_pos_match_class:
            agent_class = agent_pos_match_class.group(1)
            agent_x = int(agent_pos_match_class.group(2))
            agent_y = int(agent_pos_match_class.group(3))
            agent_pos_reliable = True
            output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from 'Yourself a {agent_class}'): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
            output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")
        else:
            # Check for cursor position if 'Yourself a [class]' is not found
            cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
            if cursor_match:
                cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
                cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))

                if is_active_prompt:
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator during active prompt). Using cursor position as best estimate for current focus: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
                    agent_x, agent_y = cursor_x, cursor_y 
                    agent_pos_reliable = True 
                else: 
                    agent_x = cursor_x
                    agent_y = cursor_y
                    agent_pos_reliable = True
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent actual position (from cursor, no 'Yourself a [class]' indicator): (x={agent_x}, y={agent_y})")
                
                agent_full_status_match = re.search(r"Agent the (\w+)\s+St:", observation_text)
                if agent_full_status_match:
                    agent_class = agent_full_status_match.group(1)
                    output_lines.append(f"Agent class: {agent_class}")

            else:
                output_lines.append("Agent actual position: Not reliably determined (missing 'Yourself a [class]' indicator and cursor coordinates).")
    else: # Agent is dead
        cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n.*?\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
        if cursor_match:
            cursor_x = int(cursor_match.group(1))
            cursor_y = int(cursor_match.group(2))
            output_lines.append(f"Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position. Cursor visible at: (x={cursor_x}, y={cursor_y}).")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Agent is dead. No reliable agent map position or cursor visible.")


    # 1. Identify items explicitly on the ground (via "You see here:")
    you_see_here_match = re.search(r"You see here:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|\n\nAgent the \w+|$)", observation_text)
    items_on_ground_explicit = []
    if you_see_here_match:
        ground_content = you_see_here_match.group(1).strip()
        if ground_content:
            ground_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", ground_content)
            for char, desc in ground_item_matches:
                items_on_ground_explicit.append(f"{char} - {desc.strip()}")
        
    if items_on_ground_explicit:
        output_lines.append(f"Items explicitly on the ground: {', '.join(items_on_ground_explicit)}")
        output_lines.append("Suggested action for ground items: pickup (to collect these items).")
    else:
        output_lines.append("No specific items explicitly found on the ground.")


    # 2. Extract items mentioned in the message and infer their location.
    items_on_ground_inferred_from_message = []

    if message_content and not is_active_prompt: 
        message_item_matches = re.findall(r"([a-zA-Z]) - ([^\.\n]+)", message_content)

        for char, desc in message_item_matches:
            candidate_item = f"{char} - {desc.strip()}"
            is_explicitly_on_ground = any(candidate_item == s for s in items_on_ground_explicit)

            if not is_explicitly_on_ground:
                items_on_ground_inferred_from_message.append(candidate_item)
        
    if items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
        output_lines.append(f"Items recognized as on the ground from message content (no active prompt): {', '.join(items_on_ground_inferred_from_message)}")
        if not items_on_ground_explicit: 
            output_lines.append("Suggested action for these items: pickup (to collect these items, if not already in inventory).")
    elif not items_on_ground_explicit: 
        output_lines.append("No specific items recognized as on the ground from general message content.")


    # 4. Check for stairs at current location based on world knowledge and infer actions
    known_stairs_location = (26, 10)
    stairs_present_at_known_location = (agent_x == known_stairs_location[0] and agent_y == known_stairs_location[1]) if agent_pos_reliable else False

    if stairs_present_at_known_location:
        descent_blocked = False
        description_suffix = ""
        if "You can't go down here" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('You can't go down here' detected)."
        elif "The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor" in message_content:
            descent_blocked = True
            description_suffix = "('The stairs are solidly fixed to the floor' detected)."
        
        if descent_blocked:
            # The more detailed message for stairs blocking is now in the general message classification.
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Blocked {description_suffix} The stairs are currently impassable.")
        else:
            output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Potentially traversable (known stairs at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}).")

        if not descent_blocked:
            if not items_on_ground_explicit and not items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: down (to descend the stairs).")
            elif items_on_ground_explicit or items_on_ground_inferred_from_message:
                output_lines.append("Suggested action: Pickup items on the ground before attempting to descend stairs.")
    else:
        if agent_pos_reliable:
             output_lines.append(f"Stairs descent status: Not at agent's current map location. Known stairs are at {known_stairs_location[0]},{known_stairs_location[1]}.")
        else:
             output_lines.append("Stairs descent status: Not at agent's reliably determined location or unknown.")


    # 5. Check for 'apply' and 'quaff' action prompts and directional prompts
    if prompt_type == "apply_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        if choices_match:
            choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
            output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'apply' action.")
    elif prompt_type == "directional_input":
        output_lines.append("Environment is prompting for directional input.")
    elif prompt_type == "quaff_item_selection":
        choices_match = re.search(r"\[([a-zA-Z*? ]+)\]", message_content)
        choices = choices_match.group(1).strip()
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is prompting for an item selection for the 'quaff' action from inventory choices: [{choices}].")
    elif prompt_type == "yes_no_quit":
        output_lines.append(f"Environment is presenting a Yes/No/Quit style prompt: '{message_content.strip()}'")


    # 6. The "Never mind." message is now handled in the main message classification.
    
    # 8. Extract identified item details from auxiliary_observation if present
    aux_obs_match = re.search(r"auxiliary_observation:\n([\s\S]*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)", observation_text)
    if aux_obs_match:
        aux_obs_content = aux_obs_match.group(1).strip()
        inventory_match = re.search(r"inventory:\n([\s\S]+)", aux_obs_content)
        if inventory_match:
            inventory_items = inventory_match.group(1).strip().split('\n')
            identified_items = []
            for item in inventory_items:
                if re.match(r"^[a-zA-Z]: .* \([\w\s]+:\d+\)$", item.strip()):
                    identified_items.append(item.strip()) 
            if identified_items:
                output_lines.append(f"Identified inventory items (from auxiliary_observation): {'; '.join(identified_items)}")
            else:
                output_lines.append("Auxiliary observation contains inventory, but no identified items.")
        else:
            output_lines.append("Auxiliary observation section found, but no inventory details within.")
    else:
        output_lines.append("No auxiliary_observation section found.")

    return "\n".join(output_lines)
```
